title: 10 best herbal creams for stye

remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Pot marigold contains californianum, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Staphylococcus aureus, helping to prevent infection and promote healing of styes.

Anti-inflammatory property: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with styes, making them less noticeable and more comfortable to manage.

Antioxidant property: Pot marigold's antioxidants help protect the skin from free radical damage, which can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, allowing the cream to promote a healthy and soothing environment for the affected area.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The soothing and calming effects of Calendula can reduce redness and swelling associated with styes, promoting a faster recovery. The antiseptic properties help combat bacterial infections that often cause styes, preventing further inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for the eye to heal. Regular application of Calendula cream can also help prevent future stye occurrences.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Calendula officinalis exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help combat the bacterial infection causing the stye.
Triterpenoids (e.g., oleanolic acid, ursolic acid): Triterpenoids in Calendula officinalis possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and pain associated with styes.

Sesquiterpene lactones (e.g., calendulin, isoalantolactone): Sesquiterpene lactones in Calendula officinalis have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate stye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and preventing infection associated with stye.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to soothe and heal the affected area.
Seeds: They are a rich source of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, aiding in the treatment of stye and promoting wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the late morning or early afternoon when the petals are fully open.
Dry the harvested flowers by spreading them in a single layer on paper bags for 1 week.
Infuse the dried flowers in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil in a 1:2 ratio for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids then add a natural emulsifier like beeswax and coconut oil.
Blend the oil mixture with distilled water and fragrance oils if desired then pour into containers for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and alocemide, help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes, promoting faster healing.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of aloe vera, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, help combat infections that may be contributing to the development or worsening of styes.

Wound-healing: Aloe vera's wound-healing properties, which include increasing collagen synthesis and improving blood flow, aid in the natural healing process of styes by promoting tissue repair and reducing scarring.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from the plant soothes the affected area, reducing redness and swelling. Its antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that cause styes, promoting a faster healing process. Additionally, aloe vera's hydrating properties keep the area moisturized, preventing further irritation and allowing the stye to heal naturally. This makes aloe vera creams a popular and effective remedy for styes.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin, a dianthrone compound, helps with stye by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness associated with styes.
Alloferon: Alloferon, a phenolic compound, has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections, such as those caused by Staphylococcus aureus, which can lead to stye formation.
Aloin: Aloin, an anthraquinone glycoside, has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that help in reducing the severity and duration of styes by modulating the body's immune response.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part of Aloe vera for making creams, as they contain high concentrations of aloe vera gel that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Seeds: The seeds are used to create oil that is rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, helping to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
(Optional) Stems: The stems are sometimes used to extract aloe vera gel, although in smaller quantities compared to leaves, as they still contain some beneficial compounds.
remedy_recipe: Melt 120 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for about 15 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of shea butter with 100 grams of coconut oil in the double boiler.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel with 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool down to around 40 degrees celsius.
Whip the mixture until it's smooth and creamy using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.

remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin around the stye.
Leaves: Echinacea angustifolia leaves are used in creams for stye due to their antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection and promote healing.

remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root and 10 grams of beeswax for the herbal cream.
Combine 30 milliliters of jojoba oil and 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Add 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of geranium essential oil to the oil mixture.
Mix 10 grams of distilled water with 2 grams of glycerin and add it to the oil mixture.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes then remove it from heat and let it cool before pouring into jars.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: The cream's antibacterial properties help to combat the underlying bacterial infection that causes a sty, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves from a reputable supplier and store them in a cool place.
Weigh out 10 grams of the dried leaves and steep them in 100 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after 5 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of cocoa butter in a double boiler and melt them after 10 minutes.
Add the strained liquid to the melted wax mixture and stir until it thickens after 5 minutes then pour it into containers.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helianthus annuus flowers at dawn when they are fully open and in full bloom.

Infuse 2 teaspoons of dried helianthus annuus flowers in 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove the solid plant material and retain the liquid.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter and 50g of coconut oil to create a base for the cream.

Steep 200g of dried lavandula flowers in 1l of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 60-70°c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes of pressure to obtain 800ml of oil.
Blend 200g of the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 50g of candelilla wax at 60-70°c for 10-15 minutes to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh geranium maculatum flowers and leaves in the morning when the dew is still present.

Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and leaves in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain 1 cup of herbal extract.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the herbal extract with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and stems from a clean and well-drained area.
Steep the dried herbs in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes in a large bowl.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the strained herbal liquid with the melted wax and oil mixture and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried silybum marianum flowers and mix with 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 50ml of sweet almond oil and heat the mixture at 70°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and let it infuse for 24 hours allowing the herbal properties to dissolve.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.
Whip the cream until it cools down slightly then store in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Gather one ounce of dried taraxacum officinale flowers and one cup of sweet almond oil.
Combine the dried flowers with two cups of boiling water and allow it steep for ten minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Add one teaspoon of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler until melted, stirring occasionally, for about twenty-five minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, then whip with an electric mixer for several minutes until creamy and smooth.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried baptisia tinctoria flowers measuring one part by weight.
Combine harvested flowers with carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in ratio of 10:90.
Steep mixture for 4-6 weeks at room temperature shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in proportions of 70:15:15 applying gently.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers and dry them thoroughly.
Combine 10 grams of dried herb with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for 24 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 30 minutes of infusing.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 50 milliliters of coconut oil and heat the mixture over low heat until melted, then combine it with the herb-infused oil and stir well.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars, let it cool, and store it in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

The antimicrobial compounds present in licorice also help combat bacterial infections that may cause styes. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which can help reduce swelling and redness associated with sty eyes.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial agents present in licorice cream, such as triterpenoids, can help combat the bacterial infection that often causes sty eyes by preventing bacterial growth and promoting healing.

Sothing and soothing agents: Licorice creams often contain soothing ingredients like glycerin, which can help calm irritated skin and reduce discomfort associated with styes, allowing for a more comfortable healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with styes.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to combat infection and reduce symptoms of styes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20-30 grams of dried licorice root.
Combine the dried licorice root with 100ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 60 minutes then filter it through cheesecloth.
Mix the filtered liquid with 10g of beeswax and 5g of coconut oil and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as hamamelitannin and gallic acid, work together to reduce swelling and redness caused by the infection. Additionally, the cream's astringent properties help to dry out the affected area, promoting the healing process and reducing the risk of further irritation or infection. By applying the cream to the affected eye or eyelid, individuals can experience relief from stye symptoms and promote faster recovery.

Gallotannins: Gallotannins have antiseptic and astringent properties, which help to dry out and heal the infected area, reducing the size and pain of the stye.
Salicylic Acid: Salicylic acid has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling, as well as combat fungal infections that can cause styes.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are rich in tannins, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with styes.
Roots: The roots contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation, making it effective for treating styes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers for a calming infusion.
Combine the dried plant material with 4 cups of distilled water in a large saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid reduces by half.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids and storing in refrigerator.
Mix one part cooled herbal infusion with three parts cold-pressed coconut oil and whip until smooth for final cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mallow creams helps with stye because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as aloe vera and chamomile, work together to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with styes. Soothing and demulcent properties: The mucilages in mallow cream create a protective barrier on the skin, soothing and calming irritated areas around the eye and reducing discomfort caused by styes.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the mallow plant contain mucilage, a natural thickening agent that helps soothe and protect the affected area.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh malva sylvestris flowers and leaves, drying them thoroughly in a warm place for at least 2 hours.
Combine 20g of dried malva sylvestris with 100ml of distilled water in a heatproof bowl, allowing it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass measuring cup, squeezing out excess liquid.
Add 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil to the filtered liquid, gently heating the mixture over low heat until melted.
Remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer until creamy and smooth, filling sterilized tubes and storing in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with stye because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness associated with this common eye infection. The cream's antibacterial compounds also combat the bacterial infection that causes the stye, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications like scarring or recurring infections. Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in stinging nettle creams protect the affected area from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy environment for healing.
Soothing and calming properties: The cream's soothing and calming effects can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with styes, making them more manageable.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin associated with styes.
Seeds: The seeds have antimicrobial properties that aid in preventing infection and promoting healing in affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems, then clean and dry them thoroughly for later use.
Combine the dried plant material with 200ml of boiling water in a saucepan and steep for 30 minutes to create an infusion.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil with the herbal infusion in a double boiler, heating until the wax melts completely.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before pouring the cream into sterilized jars and storing them in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Scottish lovage creams helps with stye because its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the affected area. The cream's active ingredients, such as berberine and mucilages, work together to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections that can cause stye. remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lovage's essential oils have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause sty infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lovage extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate the discomfort associated with sty.
Antiseptic properties: Lovage has been traditionally used to treat skin infections, including sty, due to its antiseptic properties that can help protect the skin from further bacterial invasion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are good for making medicinal creams for stye because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling.
Barks: Barks are used to make medicinal creams for stye because they contain compounds that have antibacterial and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ligusticum scoticum roots and wash them thoroughly with clean water for 30 minutes.
Dry the roots in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Melt 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of shea butter for 20-25 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the ground ligusticum scoticum powder into the melted oil mixture and stir well to combine.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for stye are natural topical treatments that help alleviate the symptoms of stye, a painful and unsightly bacterial or viral infection of the eyelid or eye margin. The best herbal creams for stye include Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea purpurea, Euphorbia peplus, Chamomilla recutita, Aloe vera, Eucalyptus globulus, Hypericum perforatum, and Plantago major. If left untreated, styes can lead to blurred vision, eye redness, swelling, and even vision loss, negatively impacting daily life, work, and social activities, causing discomfort, embarrassment, and decreased productivity.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stye that are treated with herbal creams are acne, eczema, rosacea, and dermatitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, chamomile, and aloe vera have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and heal these conditions. These natural remedies can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with these skin issues, promoting healthy and clear skin.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with stye is a blend of Tea Tree oil, Aloe Vera, and Calendula. Tea Tree oil has antibacterial properties that combat infection, while Aloe Vera soothes and calms the inflamed area. Calendula cream promotes healing and reduces inflammation, allowing the stye to resolve faster. Apply a thin layer of this combination to the affected area 2-3 times a day to alleviate discomfort and promote healing. This natural remedy can help alleviate symptoms and promote a speedy recovery.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and swelling. The antimicrobial properties also help combat bacterial infections that can cause styes. By applying Calendula creams to the affected area, it can promote a faster healing process, reduce the risk of scarring, and provide long-lasting relief from stye symptoms, promoting a healthy and clear complexion.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease the discomfort associated with a stye.
Naphthoquinones: These constituents exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help to combat bacterial infections that may have caused the stye in the first place.
Carotenoids: These antioxidants help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and promoting the healing process of the stye.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of flavonoids and triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: They are used in combination with flowers to provide a more comprehensive extract, with both parts contributing to the overall effectiveness of the cream.
Stems: They contain a lower concentration of active compounds compared to flowers and leaves, but are sometimes used to add bulk or as a cost-effective alternative.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom when petals are fully open and dried completely to preserve medicinal properties.
Infuse 250ml of carrier oil with 100g of dried calendula flowers in a clean glass jar for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil mixture through cheesecloth into a clean bowl to separate infused oil from plant material.
Combine 100g of beeswax and 200g of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt at a temperature of 180-190 degrees fahrenheit.
Mix the infused calendula oil with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture in a ratio of 1:1 to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with stye because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant reduces redness and swelling associated with styes. Its antibacterial properties combat bacterial infections that cause styes, allowing the body to heal naturally. Additionally, Aloe vera's ability to promote skin cell growth and regeneration accelerates the healing process of styes, making it an effective and natural remedy for this common eye problem. This natural remedy provides relief and comfort to those affected.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannan: Helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the affected area, which can ease the discomfort and pain associated with stye.
Saponins: Have antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacterial infections that can cause stye, reducing the risk of further complications.
Apolypodins: Possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain, promoting the healing of stye.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds such as aloe vera gel.
Seeds: Aloe seeds are used in some creams due to their rich content of antioxidants and other nutrients that can aid in healing and reducing inflammation.
Flowers: Aloe flowers are sometimes used to create aloe vera extracts that can be incorporated into creams for stye due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe gel from 3-4 mature aloe leaves by carefully cutting off the thick green skin with a knife.
Extract 1 cup of aloe gel and mix it with 1 tablespoon of distilled water in a bowl.
Combine 1/2 teaspoon of beeswax and 1/4 teaspoon of vitamin e oil in a separate bowl.
Melt the beeswax mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until fully dissolved.
Blend the aloe gel mixture with the melted beeswax mixture and pour into a glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with stye because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains tea tree oil, which has been shown to target and eliminate the bacteria that cause styes, reducing the risk of infection and inflammation. As a result, it helps to soothe and calm the affected area, promoting faster healing and reducing the discomfort associated with styes. Regular use of Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help to prevent the recurrence of styes and promote overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: An antibacterial agent that helps reduce bacterial load on the stye, preventing further infection and promoting healing.
Terpinen-4-ol: An antimicrobial agent that helps combat Staphylococcus aureus and other bacteria that can cause stye, reducing inflammation and promoting recovery.
Limonene: An antiseptic agent that helps reduce inflammation and kills bacteria, promoting a healthy environment for the stye to heal.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of tea tree oil, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat stye infections.
Leaves: Tea tree oil extracted from the leaves also helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, making it an effective ingredient in stye creams.

Leaves: The antimicrobial properties of tea tree oil from the leaves help to prevent the spread of infection and promote a faster recovery from stye, making it a popular ingredient in creams for this condition.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and set it aside for later use in the recipe.
Melt 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for about 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of shea butter and 20 grams of coconut oil in a separate heat-proof bowl.
Gradually add the shea butter and coconut oil mixture to the melted beeswax and stir until smooth and well combined.
Add the weighed melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the melted wax mixture and stir until fully incorporated.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain associated with the stye. The cream's antimicrobial properties also combat bacterial infections that cause the stye, promoting a faster healing process. Echinacea purpurea's ability to stimulate the immune system further aids in fighting off the infection, allowing the stye to heal faster and preventing further complications. remedy_constituents: Icariin: This flavonoid helps with stye by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of the affected area.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic acid helps with stye by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause infection.
Cichoric acid: This caffeoylquinic acid derivative helps with stye by reducing inflammation, preventing the spread of infection, and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds like alkylamides and glycoproteins that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and caffeic acid that help to reduce swelling and fight off infections.
Flowers: Rich in phenolic compounds and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse echinacea purpurea flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil 1:5 for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain 100ml of oil.
Melt 100g of beeswax in a double boiler and heat to 180-190°f for 10-15 minutes.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax and heat to 160-170°f for 5-10 minutes stirring constantly.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia peplus creams helps with stye because they contain a compound called ingenol mebutate, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These properties help to reduce the swelling and redness associated with a stye, while also fighting off the infection that caused it. Cembrane diterpenes: These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of soluble sap which is rich in limonoids, specifically euphorbol, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Used because they contain a concentrated form of sap which can be extracted and used in creams to target stye infections.
Leaves: Used as well because they have the ability to promote a quick and effective recovery from stye infections by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of euphorbia peplus roots and leaves, clean them thoroughly under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours using a food dehydrator.
Grind 10g of dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of the ground plant powder with 50g of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 15 minutes, then let it cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream reduces redness and swelling, allowing the stye to heal faster. Chamomilla recutita, also known as German chamomile, contains apigenin and bisabolol, which have antimicrobial effects. These properties help combat the bacterial infection causing the stye, promoting a quick recovery. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties keep the eye area hydrated, making it an effective treatment for styes.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that reduces inflammation and soothes the skin, helping to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with styes.
Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that helps to reduce the swelling and prevent infection of the stye.
α-Bisabolol oxide B: A sesquiterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help to reduce the severity of stye symptoms and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm the affected area and reduce swelling.
Leaves: They are used for their antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection and promote the healing of the stye.
Seeds: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain associated with the stye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried flowers in the early morning or late afternoon when their volatile oils are most concentrated.
Infuse 250g of the dried flowers in 500ml of cold-pressed carrier oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container.
Add 50g of beeswax and 50g of candelilla wax to the infused oil and melt at 180-190°f for 10 minutes.
Pour the melted wax mixture into small containers and let them cool and harden for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, effectively reducing redness and swelling associated with styes. It also promotes wound healing and helps to keep the area clean, preventing further bacterial growth. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of Aloe vera soothe and calm the affected eye, providing relief from discomfort and pain. This makes Aloe vera creams an effective remedy for stye.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: They help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that can cause stye, promoting faster healing and recovery.
Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe the affected area, reduce pain, and prevent further bacterial growth.
Flavonoids: They possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote the healing process of the stye, ultimately preventing its recurrence.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is often used in creams to treat stye due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Seeds: Aloe vera seeds contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them a useful ingredient in stye creams.
Barks: Aloe vera bark extracts are sometimes used in creams to treat stye due to their potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather all the ingredients which include 1 cup aloe vera gel 2 tablespoons coconut oil 2 tablespoons beeswax and 2 teaspoons vitamin e oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for about 10 minutes.
Add the aloe vera gel to the melted oil mixture and stir well to combine thoroughly.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency suitable for whipping.
Whip the mixture until it becomes light and fluffy and then transfer it to a container for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compound, eucalyptol, has been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with styes. The cooling sensation provided by the cream can also help to bring the stye to a head, allowing it to drain and heal more quickly. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of eucalyptus globulus help to combat infection and promote the healing process. This leads to faster recovery and reduced risk of complications.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It helps reduce inflammation and has antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its effectiveness in treating stye.
Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in soothing the affected area and reducing pain associated with stye.
Terpenes (Caryophyllene, Limonene): They exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which aid in combating bacterial infections and reducing inflammation in stye.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and decongestant properties to soothe and reduce eye inflammation.
Barks: Utilized for their astringent, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce swelling and fight off infections.

Essential Oil from the Leaves: Comprising of cineole and other compounds, it is used to penetrate deep into the skin and provide anti-inflammatory and antibacterial benefits to treat stye effectively.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal potency and minimal contamination.
Extract the essential oil from eucalyptus globulus leaves using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin in a ratio of 1:5.
Mix the extracted essential oil with a carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil in a ratio of 10% to 20%.
Combine the essential oil mixture with a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax in a ratio of 1:4.
Whip the mixture using a hand mixer or blender until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency suitable for topical application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with stye because they contain the active ingredient hypericin, which has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and viral infections that cause sty. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling and pain associated with sty. Additionally, hypericin's antiseptic properties promote wound healing and prevent further infection, allowing the sty to heal faster. This makes Hypericum perforatum creams a popular natural remedy for treating styes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with stye by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the affected area, which can aid in the healing process and reduce the severity of symptoms.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Hypericum perforatum creams have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may be causing or contributing to the stye.
Naphthodianthrones: Naphthodianthrones exhibit antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing the swelling and redness associated with styes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their flavonoid and phenolic content, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties effective against stye.
Flowers: Utilized for their flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are thought to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate stye symptoms.
Stems: Employed due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which may contribute to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, beneficial for treating stye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a sunny location to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm air-circulating environment at 30-40 degrees celsius for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum with 500 grams of base cream in a stainless steel mixing vessel.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler setup at 60-70 degrees celsius for 30-40 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Strain the hot mixture through a fine-mesh filter into a clean container and allow it to cool before application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago major creams helps with stye because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition. The cream's soothing and calming effects also help to ease the pain and discomfort caused by a stye. Additionally, Plantago major's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause styes, promoting a faster recovery and preventing the stye from worsening. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution for soothing and healing styes.
remedy_constituents: Apolipoprotein: Helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the affected area, contributing to the resolution of stye symptoms.
Saponins: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may be causing the stye, thereby aiding in its treatment.
Flavonoids: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort associated with stye.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for stye due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Seeds: Utilized in creams for stye due to their antiseptic and soothing effects on the affected area.
Roots: Employed in creams for stye due to their anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which help reduce swelling and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30 grams of dried plantago major leaves in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids immediately.
Combine the infused liquid with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat while stirring occasionally for 20 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container.
